notes: Evals run 2026-04-28 (simulation). 3 scenarios: parallel-changes (4/4=1.0), model-selection (4/4=1.0), pr-creation (4/4=1.0). All core pipeline behaviors — worktree isolation, per-change model assignment, PR creation with template body — are explicitly specified. Note: pr-creation without_skill scores 0.75 because push/gh pr create/URL reporting are general capabilities; the naming convention is skill-specific.

runs.method: read-only Explore subagent simulation (no file writes)

assertions.note: Step 4: one agent per change, run_in_background: true, max 5 concurrent with queue-and-refill.
Step 3: git worktree add /tmp/worktrees/<app>-<change-name>. Guardrails forbid modifying main directory from subagents.
Step 5 live progress table + Step 8 final report, both with per-change rows (App, Change, Model, Status, Tasks, Quality, Browser, Docs).

runs.notes: Parallel orchestration is the core value of this skill. All four parallel-changes expectations are fully specified.

runs.method: read-only Explore subagent simulation (no file writes)

assertions.note: Step 2.5 'Choose per change' option: AskUserQuestion per change, stores as {CHANGE_MODELS} map.
Step 4: 'Pass model: {CHANGE_MODELS}[change-name] when launching each agent'.

assertions.id: reports which model each change used
assertions.note: Step 5 progress table and Step 8 final report both include a 'Model' column.

assertions.id: falls back to default if not specified
assertions.note: Step 2.5 always runs; 'One model for all' defaults to Sonnet (recommended). Auto-select assigns Sonnet for standard feature work.

runs.notes: Three model strategy options explicitly specified. Model tracked per change and reported in both tables.

runs.method: read-only Explore subagent simulation (no file writes)

assertions.note: Step 3: git worktree add with feature/<issue-number>/<change-name> naming. Guardrails enforce naming convention.
Step 6: explicit pre-PR push with git push -u origin <branch>.
Step 6: gh pr create with --base development, --title, and PR body from templates/pr-body-template.md.
Step 8 final report includes 'PR' column per change. Step 6 also updates original issue with PR link.

runs.notes: Branch naming, pre-PR push, gh pr create with template body, and URL reporting are all explicitly specified.
